Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team

-- 2 of 9 --

ADAPTING YOUR SCRIPT
FOR YOUR INDUSTRY
H-;'(1#9"8%#+3%'$&#4&#9"8%#+-/.+#+'&8-&'"(#'+#"(.#&*'(12#78&#&*.#$"'(&+#7./"=#
='//#.(+8%.#&*-&#9"8%#+3%'$&#D"=+#='&*#9"8%#'(08+&%9#'(#-#+.-,/.++#)-+*'"(6
! 	! 	!H-'-*:$%-!@()'!'(-!#+-/$1/!G3..-'!+,$%'#!)*-!=,*!'(-!$%23#'*5!
in question.
Determine the correct person you need to contact.
For example, sometimes you‚Äôre calling the CEO and sometimes
your calling the Purchasing Manager. You know you need both
of them on board to get your product in the door, so if you can
get the CEO to not necessarily say ‚Äúyes‚Äù to the product, but to
become educated to a level of certainty on your product, then
when you get into contact with the Purchasing Manager, he or she
is already preframed to say ‚Äúyes.‚Äù
Use the language of the industry.
If you‚Äôre selling stocks or insurance, you‚Äôre going to want to sound
like a Financial Expert. However, if you‚Äôre selling solar energy,
9"8#,-9#=-(&#&"#,-;.#-#+")&.%2#1%..(.%#+-/.6##:+'(1#&*.#3"%%.3&#
language of the industry to which you‚Äôre selling is crucial!
1
2
3
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	2

-- 3 of 9 --

Create a sense of urgency or scarcity while remaining ethical.
Some industries lend themselves to scarcity or urgency. In order
to create that scarcity or urgency, you could create shortterm
programs or ‚Äúdeals.‚Äù Every 5 days your program may change,
thus your salesforce can say you have 5 days to snatch up this
deal before it‚Äôs over. And truly, that will be the case. After 5
days of one deal, create a new deal. Your salesforce will have to
change their scripts accordingly, but will be able to so honestly.
:+'(1#&*.+.#&-3&'3+#&"#-0-$&#9"8%#+3%'$&#&"#+$.3'43#'(08+&%'.+#='//#-//"=#
you to tailor your presentation toward a successful close no matter what
business you‚Äôre in or what sale you are pursuing.
But how do you ensure that your entire salesforce knows what script to
use when? You must train them in Script Building Technology.
4
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	3

-- 4 of 9 --

HOW TO TRAIN
YOUR SALES TEAM
N(3.#9"8@5.#4(-/'C.0#9"8%#+3%'$&2#&*.%.#-%.#"(/9#&="#&*'(1+#&"#0"#='&*#9"8%#
team: (1) train, train, train, and (2) drill, drill, drill.
You want your entire team to get to a level of unconscious competence,
where the script is so much a part of their nature that they don‚Äôt have to
expend any energy thinking about what to say next. Instead, they can
use their mental energy to look past their client‚Äôs actual words, therefore
leading him or her more elegantly down the Straight Line.
Roleplaying and practicing consistently and constantly is a great way
to keep people from veering off the script. Scripts are proven to take a
person from A to Z smoothly, elegantly, accurately, and ethically. There
will be some veering off strict scripts in the second half of the Straight
Line to tailor the script to a particular client‚Äôs needs or rebuttals, but they
should be encouraged to always stick to the script.
And, it is critical that your team members not sound like they are reading
from a script‚Äîthey must sound natural. You must help them understand
that it‚Äôs impossible to do too much drilling. Have your team split up into
small groups of 24 people to drill each other every morning. After a short
$.%'"0#")#&',.#9"8#='//#0.4('&./9#7.1'(#&"#+..#%.,-%;-7/.#',$%"5.,.(&+#'(#
the quality of their presentations.
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	4

-- 5 of 9 --

Now, as they engrain their scripts into their way of life, the question
presents itself, ‚ÄúIs there a difference in what scripts you use for different
types of sales calls?‚Äù
The Difference in Sales Calls
The true difference between sales calls is where you enter on the Straight
Line. Your script‚Äôs introductory paragraph and language patterns will be
different depending on where your lead came from.
Inbound Calls vs. Cold Calls
If you receive an Inbound Call, the person will typically say, ‚ÄúHi, I‚Äôm
interested in this.‚Äù So you say, ‚ÄúOkay, great. Where did you read about
8+V[##E(0#&*.(#9"8@//#+-92#Z>.%%'436##h8+&#-#3"8$/.#I8'3;#I8.+&'"(+#+"#<#
don‚Äôt waste your time and can best serve you.‚Äù When you receive an
Inbound Call, the person is basically already interested, right? They‚Äôve
already been marketed to versus a Cold Call where you‚Äôre essentially
marketing to them on the phone. With an inbound call, you already know
the person is interested in your product so you‚Äôre going to enter the
Straight Line a bit further toward the close than with the Cold Call.
In a Cold Call Script or a Telemarketing Script‚Äîthe operative word being
Z,-%;.&'(1[\&*.#4%+&#$-%-1%-$*#'+#%.-//9#-7"8&#+..'(1#')#&*.9@%.#.5.(#
'(&.%.+&.06##B"#"(3.#9"8#1.&#&*%"81*#&*.#4%+&#$-%-1%-$*#")#.'&*.%#&*.#
inbound call or the cold call, you‚Äôre basically at the same spot on the
Straight Line‚Äîthe qualifying section.
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	5

-- 6 of 9 --

Calling Back a Lead
When you‚Äôre Calling Back a Lead, they already have a certain amount
of information about your product or they wouldn‚Äôt be a lead, they‚Äôre
$%.I8-/'4.0#+"#9"8#0"(@&#*-5.#&"#$.%)"%,#&*-&#$-%&#")#9"8%#+3%'$&6
What you‚Äôre doing now is essentially saying, ‚ÄúIf you recall, we spoke
back on this day.‚Äù And then you say, ‚ÄúThe reason for the call is that‚Ä¶‚Äù
and this is where you come up with your sense of urgency, your reason
for calling today.
Then, you‚Äôre back on the Straight Line.
Typically, what I do in those scripts is requalify them to create consistency.
If they said they need the service and are looking for Xamount of
customers or they have Xamount of money to invest, I would repeat that
'()"%,-&'"(#)"%#3"(4%,-&'"(#7.)"%.#<#=.(&#'(&"#,9#$'&3*6
Now, let‚Äôs take a few moments to review the most important elements of
script delivery.
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	6

-- 7 of 9 --

EFFECTIVE SCRIPT DELIVERY:
A REVIEW
Use effective tonality.
All the different ways you manage your voice to keep a client
interested, to keep them hanging on your every word, to make
them feel things unconsciously with just the tone of your voice,
to sound 100% natural. You‚Äôll need to acquire different tonalities
for different situations, e.g., your reasonable person tone, your
nobigdeal tone, your tone of scarcity, etc.
Use genuine responses.
When you ask someone how they‚Äôre doing, and they respond with
‚Äúokay,‚Äù you‚Äôre response should be genuine and caring. A genuine
response will help you keep rapport with your client.
Roleplay your script until it is seamless.
Before you ever unleash your script on the public, roleplay the script
8(&'/#'&#'+#+.-,/.++2#D"='(12#-(0#(-&8%-/6##J"";#)"%#+&'3;'(1#$"'(&+2#
&"(18.G&='+&.%+2#.F-11.%-&'"(+2#-(0#-(9&*'(1#./+.#&*-&#7/"3;+#&*.#D"=#
of your content or the way you say it.
1
2
3
4Qualify your leads.
Qualify your leads so you don‚Äôt waste time pitching people who
="8/0#(.5.%#*-5.#7"81*&#9"8%#$%"083&#'(#&*.#4%+&#$/-3.6
Don‚Äôt make your scripts too general.
They make their scripts too general just to get people in their
pipeline. But, they‚Äôre just wasting their time because those people
-%.#8(I8-/'4.0#-(0#&*8+#&*.#+-/.+$.%+"(#="(@&#7.#-7/.#&"#,-;.#
the close.
5
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team	7

-- 8 of 9 --

Don‚Äôt frontload your scripts.
7
They frontload their scripts and then there arsenal is depleted
when they get to a negative response. They have nothing to
combat anything but a ‚Äúyes.‚Äù
! 	! 	I--+!'(-!2$).,&3-!?,@$%&4
Not allowing for rapport. The salesperson preaches to the client
instead of inserting opportunities for the client to respond.
6
JORDAN BELFORT
Adapting Your Script & Training Your Sales Team
